informative.title: Pan-Tompkins-style ECG R-wave detection
informative.summary: Detects ordered R-wave occurrence times from a sampled ECG signal and exposes intermediate processing signals for plotting and debugging.
informative.description: This Pan-Tompkins-style detector is implemented in Biosigmat using bandpass filtering, derivative filtering, squaring, moving-window integration, peak detection, and peak refinement. The current implementation follows the Pan-Tompkins processing style but is not a byte-for-byte reproduction of the original paper.
notes: The primary detection target is the ECG R wave.
Intermediate outputs are part of the public contract because they are used for plotting and debugging detections.
Exact cross-language numerical equality of intermediate signals is not required by the first positive conformance case.
Python should expose r_wave_times, ecg_filtered, decg, and decg_envelope using the canonical names.
Insufficient-data behavior remains unspecified in this draft.

references.note: Algorithm origin for the Pan-Tompkins-style processing chain.

definitions.text: Apply bandpass filtering, derivative filtering, squaring, moving-window integration, peak detection, and peak refinement to the ECG signal.
Detected ECG R-wave occurrence times in seconds, sorted in ascending order.
Bandpass-filtered ECG signal, represented as a one-dimensional vector with the same canonical sample order and length as the input ECG.
Derivative-filtered ECG signal, represented as a one-dimensional vector with the same canonical sample order and length as the input ECG.
Squared and moving-window integrated detection envelope, represented as a one-dimensional vector with the same canonical sample order and length as the input ECG.

behavior.nan_handling: NaN samples in the ECG signal are preserved through filtering as the implementation permits. Detections should not be produced inside NaN-corrupted regions in a future shared NaN case; the case in this draft contains no NaN samples.
behavior.empty_input: Empty ECG input is invalid; the exact failure mechanism is implementation-specific.
behavior.input_orientation: Treat ECG input as a one-dimensional vector regardless of row or column orientation. All vector outputs are conceptually one-dimensional ordered vectors.
